nodes.master_summary: The structural safeguard against pure-Construct existence. Connecting body to mind kept Jovians tethered to physical reality and locked their brains into the Construct's compute substrate. Violations of this law underwrite most of the canonical Jove failure modes that follow.

nodes.name: The Deathless
nodes.brief: Mysterious figure first surfacing YC110 era. Operates the Deathless Network, distributes intel and resources across pirate factions, currently seen pulling Archbine away from Mordu's Legion / Mu and into his own circle.
nodes.master_summary: The current cycle's antagonist-of-record. Modern Deathless arc converges multiple long-running threads: stolen capsuleer-grade resources, unknown connection to Jove deep history (the name + immortality framing reads as Construct-derived), Pochven operations, Archbine defection. Counter-target of Intaki Bank / Upwell counter-insurgency.

nodes.name: Deathless Network
nodes.brief: Distribution arm of the Deathless. Seeds intel and resources to pirate factions; documented role in distributing capsuleer-grade resources stolen by Esri-class operations to other pirate groups.
nodes.master_summary: The Deathless Network is the operational layer between the Deathless himself and the pirate ecosystem. Functions as a black-market intel exchange — same shape as the Antagonists-chronicle Sentona/Lacante/Ishukone data trade, but with the Deathless rather than Ishukone as the terminal recipient. Where Intaki Bank's counter-insurgency is aimed.

nodes.name: Thera
nodes.brief: Wormhole-space (Anoikis) system. Sisters of EVE's secret research base, revealed publicly during the Drifter-era reveal. Staging point for SoE / Drifter / Triglavian research.
nodes.master_summary: The Sisters of EVE's deep-research base, hidden in Anoikis until externally discovered. Sentona Sarpati's organization runs its non-humanitarian work from here. Structurally adjacent to every Drifter / Sleeper / hyperspace-network thread — the SoE-AIR-EDENCOM research pipeline runs through this site. Silphy's SoE training era likely intersected the Thera operation, though the published chronicles don't place her there directly.

nodes.name: Muryia Mordu
nodes.brief: Founder of Mordu's Legion. Implicit referent in the Antagonists chronicle's 'mercenaries' line — the unnamed commander Silphy works around in her Ishukone data trade.
nodes.master_summary: Mordu's Legion's founder. Independent mercenary commander operating outside the four-empire structure. Antagonists-chronicle subtext: the 'mercenaries' Silphy negotiates around when offering her Zephyr-telemetry data to Ishukone — Mens won't take the deal directly while Mordu's people are in the way. Mordu's Legion's separate relationship to Ishukone via the Templar One clone-soldier program ties him structurally to the Mens-Reppola axis.

nodes.name: Caldari Prime
nodes.brief: Original Caldari homeworld in the Luminaire system. Orbitally bombarded by Gallente during the Caldari Rebellion; the surviving exiles founded New Caldari Prime. Recovered by Tibus Heth's Provost coup; later re-administered under Federation oversight.
nodes.master_summary: The lost homeworld at the heart of the Caldari-Gallente schism. Cyclically contested across centuries — bombarded by Gallente in the Rebellion, abandoned by surviving Caldari, reclaimed by Heth in the Empyrean Age, then re-administered diplomatically. Caldari Prime is the symbolic counterweight to New Caldari Prime: one is the lost mother, the other the chosen successor. Every Caldari–Gallente political beat references this duality. Heth's coup gave him the political capital to absorb Ytiri and reshape mega-corp alignment; without Caldari Prime there is no Provost era and no Mountain-vs-Forest realignment.

nodes.name: Tibus Heth
nodes.brief: Caldari populist Provost. Rose from Kaalakiota blue-collar leadership to lead the coup that reclaimed Caldari Prime, then forged the Provost alliance that absorbed Ytiri and remade Caldari mega-corp politics for ~5 years.
nodes.master_summary: The most disruptive Caldari political figure of the modern era. Heth's reclamation of Caldari Prime turned populist anger into a cross-corp alliance — the Provost — that swept aside the post-rebellion mega-corp consensus. Under Heth, Ytiri was absorbed into the alliance (taking Star Seed onto the Ishukone-axis side), Sukuuvestaa pushed back, and the State briefly operated as a single political bloc. His fall ~YC115 left a power vacuum that the Mountain + Ocean factions filled, completing the Lai-Dai-vs-Ishukone realignment Ash now diagrams.

nodes.name: Kaalakiota Corporation
nodes.brief: Caldari mega-corp specializing in heavy industry and weapons manufacturing. Tibus Heth's home corporation; the Provost alliance's industrial spine.
nodes.master_summary: One of the eight Caldari mega-corps. Kaalakiota's manufacturing base was the political launching pad for the Provost alliance — Heth used the corp's blue-collar workforce as a populist constituency. Post-Heth, Kaalakiota settled into the Mountain-faction realignment alongside Ishukone. Its weapons output makes it the State's primary military-industrial supplier, structurally similar to Lai Dai's commercial position but with a more austere internal culture.

nodes.name: Sukuuvestaa Corporation
nodes.brief: Largest Caldari mega-corp by revenue. Insurance, heavy industry, and agricultural conglomerate. Politically conservative; Heth-era resistor of Provost absorption.
nodes.master_summary: The Caldari State's economic backbone. Sukuuvestaa runs the largest civilian workforce of any Caldari corporation, which gives it political weight without needing Provost-style coalitions. Resisted Heth's absorption attempts; emerged from the Provost era institutionally intact, which made it the natural anchor of the post-Heth Mountain faction. Insurance arm is the State's structural memory — Sukuuvestaa records every claim made against every other mega-corp's operations.

nodes.name: Hyasyoda Corporation
nodes.brief: Caldari mega-corp specializing in agriculture, biotech, and consumer goods. Forest-faction-aligned per Ash's whiteboard.
nodes.master_summary: The State's agricultural and biotech anchor. Less politically visible than Ishukone or Lai Dai, but holds the food-supply leverage that becomes critical when border restrictions tighten — which is exactly what happened during the Jita-gate post-incident curfews. Forest-faction-aligned, making Hyasyoda a Lai Dai-axis sister-corp on Ash's structural read of Caldari politics.

nodes.name: Wiyrkomi Corporation
nodes.brief: Caldari mega-corp specializing in shipbuilding and naval engineering. Builds the Wyvern supercarrier and other State flagships.
nodes.master_summary: The Caldari Navy's primary capital-ship builder. Wiyrkomi's Wyvern-class line is the structural anchor of the State's super-fleet doctrine; loss of a Wiyrkomi yard is a strategic event. Mountain-faction-aligned and tightly coordinated with Kaalakiota's munitions output — the two together form the Mountain/Ocean military-industrial cluster that the Forest faction (Lai Dai-led commercial trade) doesn't have a direct counter for.

nodes.name: Tash-Murkon (region)
nodes.brief: Amarr region under House Tash-Murkon's stewardship. Youngest of the five royal-house regions. Adjacent to the Khanid Kingdom and Aridia.
nodes.master_summary: The most progressive Amarr royal region — House Tash-Murkon is the only Udorian-bloodline royal house and is structurally less conservative on slave reform than Sarum, Kor-Azor, Kador, or Ardishapur. The region's geography puts it adjacent to Khanid (the defected kingdom) and Aridia (the lawless border), which historically positions Tash-Murkon as the empire's softest political edge. Mordu's Legion runs significant operations here.

nodes.name: Sebiestor Tribe
nodes.brief: One of the seven Mimitar tribes. Engineering-focused; the Republic's industrial backbone. The Sebiestor Tribe corporation is the political face within Republic governance.
nodes.master_summary: The Mimitar's engineering tribe — produces the bulk of Republic naval shipwrights, capsule technologists, and infrastructure engineers. Less mystically-inclined than the Vherokior, less martial than the Brutor or Krusual; the structural counterweight inside the Tribal Council that keeps the Republic's industrial pipeline running. Most likely candidate for a Mimitar-side Mil-Campaign zone after Starkmanir, given its visibility in the Republic's modern self-image.

nodes.name: Angel Cartel
nodes.brief: Major pirate faction operating from Curse, Great Wildlands, and Stain-adjacent space. Loose criminal alliance with deep ties to Mordu's Legion's founding personnel.
nodes.master_summary: Southern New Eden's enduring pirate organization. Angel Cartel financing has been documented in multiple Caldari–Gallente political destabilization events; the four-empire posture toward them is performatively hostile, structurally tolerant. Mordu's Legion's founding membership came from Angel Cartel deserters — the relationship between the two is more genealogical than antagonistic. Skarkon's defection to Cartel administration ~YC110 is the canonical demonstration of how an empire-region falls when the Republic stops servicing it.

nodes.name: Intaki
nodes.brief: Federation-member people; one of the founding Gallente bloodlines. Distinct from the Intaki Syndicate (the dissident exile group that retained the name in nullsec). Religious tradition: Ida faith with reincarnation belief.
nodes.master_summary: The original Intaki bloodline — peaceful, philosophical, religiously distinct via the Ida faith's reincarnation cosmology. Founding members of the Federation, but always retained cultural autonomy that produced both the Caldari-sympathetic exile (the Syndicate) and the Lost-Adama religious arc (Eloa Enaro / Negui Erlon, YC120). The Federation's seizure of Intaki in YC124 — what triggered the Syndicate–Caldari constellation deal — was an act against this people, not against the Syndicate. Moreau's amnesty offer post-conquest is structurally an attempt to reach the Intaki proper, not the Syndicate fugitives.

nodes.name: Nefantar Tribe
nodes.brief: The Mimitar tribe that defected to the Amarr during the Day of Darkness, renamed 'Ammatar' under Damyas III's honorific. Restored to Republic membership in YC115 under Alika Valkanir; many descendants still administer the Ammatar Mandate.
nodes.master_summary: The Nefantar are the Mimitar tribe whose Day-of-Darkness collaboration produced the Ammatar Mandate. The Imperian-Age reveal showed they had been covertly hiding Starkmanir survivors during the centuries of Amarr rule — partial redemption that recategorized them from outright traitors to long-game protectors. Restored as the seventh Mimitar tribe in YC115. The split between 'Nefantar' (tribal identity, Republic-restored) and 'Ammatar' (mandate-administering descendants still loyal to the Empire) is the structural backbone of any future Mil-Campaign #2 zone.

nodes.name: Brutor Tribe
nodes.brief: Mimitar warrior tribe. Most martially-inclined of the seven; the Republic's primary infantry / capsuleer-pilot recruitment pool. Brutor Chief Ukumi Pol named the Amarr explicitly during the Jita-gate crisis.
nodes.master_summary: The Mimitar's warrior tribe — produces the bulk of Republic naval officers, ground-force infantry, and capsuleer pilots. Politically the most hawkish tribal voice. Brutor Chief Ukumi Pol's YC128 statement pointing fingers at the Amarr is consistent with Brutor's longstanding posture: the tribe has never bought into the appeasement cycles that Krusual or Sebiestor leadership periodically pursue.

nodes.name: Krusual Tribe
nodes.brief: Mimitar mountain-people tribe. Politically conservative, traditional, and the source of the 'Krusual' (the unbroken-Mimitar) cultural identity that resisted full Amarr enslavement.
nodes.master_summary: The Mimitar's mountain tribe — geographically isolated during the Amarr era, which preserved more of the pre-Empire culture than any other Republic tribe retains. Politically conservative and resistant to Galante-style democratic reform. The Krusoral identity (which Ash highlights) is the Krusual self-conception: the unbent, unbroken, never-fully-enslaved Mimitar lineage.

nodes.name: Black Rise
nodes.brief: Caldari frontier nullsec region adjacent to The Forge and Lonetrek. Lai Dai administrative target during the Black Rise development push (~YC110). Faction-warfare zone with Gallente.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari's eastern frontier region. Lai Dai's announced 'Black Rise development' was a major mid-2010s commercial-expansion play that put the corp's resources into the borderland. Its FW status against Gallente makes it the eastern counterpart to Placid (the western FW border) — together they bracket the Caldari–Gallente conflict zone that Warpath Protocol Failure exposed.

nodes.name: Molden Heath
nodes.brief: Mimitar frontier region. Adjacent to Heimatar and Great Wildlands. Krusual / Brutor military operations zone; structurally the Republic's eastern flank.
nodes.master_summary: Republic frontier region. Molden Heath is where the Republic's traditional military architecture (Brutor / Krusual deployments) projects power outward. Adjacent to Great Wildlands, which Thukker semi-autonomously administers, making the Molden Heath ↔ Great Wildlands border the Republic's tribal-to-tribal handoff. Ash flags Molden Heath as a possible Mimitar-side Mil-Campaign zone if the Ammatar Mandate prediction plays out.

nodes.name: Great Wildlands
nodes.brief: NPC-nullsec region. Thukker tribal territory; Mordu's Legion operations zone. Closest Mimitar analogue to the Intaki Syndicate's NPC-nullsec status in Syndicate region.
nodes.master_summary: The Mimitar tribal frontier at NPC scale. Thukker administers; Mordu's Legion operates from. Structurally the tribe's parallel to Intaki Syndicate's relationship with the Federation — semi-autonomous, NPC-controlled, semi-deniable. Prismatic / fractured-site activity has been heavy here during the Catalyst era. Drifter-tech research that the Republic-proper doesn't want to claim happens through Great Wildlands intermediaries.

nodes.name: Caille
nodes.brief: Capital city of the Gallente Federation, located on Gallente Prime. Seat of the Federation Senate, Presidential office, and Yulai-aligned diplomatic infrastructure.
nodes.master_summary: Gallente Prime's capital city — political center of the Federation. Senatorial nominations, presidential inaugurations, and the Yulai diplomatic apparatus all anchor here. Moreau's YC128 inauguration was held in Caille under the Jita-gate cloud. Symbolic counterweight to New Caldari Prime (Caldari political center) and Amarr (Empire center) — the three-empire political-capital geography is one of CCP's clearest storytelling anchors.

nodes.name: Caldari Business Tribunal
nodes.brief: Caldari judicial / regulatory body. Investigated the financial corruption operating out of Jita that — per Saraki's CEP statement — turned out to be Drifter-hyperspace-network manipulation, not conventional fraud.
nodes.master_summary: The CBT is the Caldari State's mega-corp regulator. Long-running investigation of Jita financial corruption was the diplomatic cover under which Saraki revealed (during the post-Jita-gate CEP statement) that the 'corruption' was actually weaponized hyperspace-network exploitation. CBT operates in tension with the House of Records: where the latter tracks raw transaction history, the CBT decides what's legal. Forest-faction-aligned per the structural pattern (Lai-Dai-administered commerce gets the regulatory seat).

nodes.name: Sansha's Nation
nodes.brief: Cybernetic-pirate empire founded by Sansha Kuvakei. True-slave neural-conditioning technology. Periodic incursion campaigns against high-sec systems; structural counterpart to the Drifter / Triglavian incursion patterns.
nodes.master_summary: The fourth major canonical pirate empire — Sansha's signature is the True-Slave neural conditioning, an extreme of the Vitoxin / mind-control technology spectrum. Periodic incursions historically targeted both high-sec and faction territory; many of CCP's earlier expansion arcs (incursion mechanic, response protocols) were shaped around Sansha's behavior pattern. Genealogically related to the Sarpati line through Sansha Kuvakei's family — one of the few canonical bridges between the southern pirate factions and the Sarpati black-market network.

nodes.name: Heideran VII
nodes.brief: Amarr Emperor who preceded Doriam Kor-Azor. Long-reigning, conservative; presided over the Empire during the early modern era of capsuleer activity.
nodes.master_summary: The pre-modern Amarr throne anchor. Heideran's death triggered the championship that produced Doriam Kor-Azor — the succession that Karsoth later subverted. Heideran's long reign is the structural reason the Empire's political institutions are as conservative as they are: his persistence prevented the kind of generational turnover the other empires got, leaving the Amarr political system unusually rigid by the time the Empyrean Age destabilization hit.

nodes.name: Doriam Kor-Azor
nodes.brief: Doriam II. Amarr Emperor between Heideran VII and Jamyl I. Reformist; assassinated under circumstances that put Karsoth into the Provost Marshal seat and effectively ran the Empire from there.
nodes.master_summary: Doriam Kor-Azor was the brief reformist throne between Heideran's long conservatism and Jamyl's superweapon-defined reign. His assassination is one of the most consequential political events of the modern era — it left Karsoth (a Provost Marshal) in operational control of the Empire while the Royal Heirs were unable to consolidate succession. The Karsoth interregnum is when most of the structural Amarr-vs-Republic instability that fed into the Imperian Age was set up.

nodes.name: Aritcio Kor-Azor
nodes.brief: House Kor-Azor heir, son of Doriam II. Implicated in the corrupt-Provost Marshal era; House Kor-Azor's reputation took a long-term hit through this line.
nodes.master_summary: The Kor-Azor heir whose involvement in Provost Marshal politics tarnished the house's standing through the post-Doriam interregnum. Structurally important as the named successor whose corruption became the explicit reason House Kor-Azor lost political weight relative to Sarum and Tash-Murkon during Jamyl's rise. Modern Amarr royal politics still references the Kor-Azor diminishment as a cautionary precedent.

nodes.name: Karsoth
nodes.brief: Provost Marshal who took de facto control of the Amarr Empire after Doriam Kor-Azor's assassination. Opposed Royal Heir succession until Jamyl Sarum's emergence forced the championship.
nodes.master_summary: The Karsoth interregnum is the modern Amarr's structural disaster — a Provost Marshal running the Empire's day-to-day while the throne sat vacant. His refusal to concede succession to the Royal Heirs is the proximate cause of much of the Empire's mid-modern instability and explains why Jamyl I's emergence was framed as restoration rather than coronation. Karsoth's tenure is also the structural ancestor of the kind of office-holder-vs-throne tension that any modern Amarr political beat references.

nodes.name: House Sarum
nodes.brief: One of the five Amarr royal houses. The most martial / military-traditionalist of the five; produced Jamyl Sarum (Empress Jamyl I).
nodes.master_summary: House Sarum's institutional identity is military sufficiency. Their territorial seat is centered on Sarum Prime (destroyed by Jamyl's superweapon during the Elder Fleet attack — an in-house event with massive symbolic weight). Sarum's combat-doctrine focus made it the natural counterweight to Karsoth's bureaucratic interregnum, and Jamyl's emergence from this house reset the Empire's institutional center of gravity for a generation.

nodes.name: House Tash-Murkon
nodes.brief: The youngest of the five Amarr royal houses, and the only one of Udorian rather than Amarr bloodline. Stewards the Tash-Murkon Region (territorial seat). Politically the empire's softest edge.
nodes.master_summary: House Tash-Murkon's distinctness is structural — Udorian rather than Amarr bloodline means it operates inside the Amarr political system without quite being of it. The youngest house, the most progressive on slave reform, and the natural diplomatic interlocutor when Empire–Federation or Empire–Republic talks need a non-confrontational Amarr counterparty. Tash-Murkon's region is also the Empire's southern frontier with Khanid (defected) and Aridia (lawless), giving the house operational reasons to maintain pragmatic borders.

nodes.name: House Kor-Azor
nodes.brief: One of the five Amarr royal houses. Doriam Kor-Azor's house. Reformist tradition tarnished by Aritcio's Provost-era corruption.
nodes.master_summary: House Kor-Azor's institutional identity is reformist: Doriam II's brief reign was the modern Empire's last significant attempt at reform-from-the-throne. The house's reputation took a long-term hit when Aritcio's involvement in the Karsoth-era corrupt Provost Marshal apparatus tarnished the lineage. Kor-Azor's reduced political weight relative to Sarum and Tash-Murkon during Jamyl's rise is the structural memory of that failure — and the cautionary precedent every future Amarr reformist program references.

nodes.name: House Kador
nodes.brief: One of the five Amarr royal houses. The most conservative slaver-traditionalist of the five. Hardline on the religious-political axis.
nodes.master_summary: House Kador anchors the conservative end of Amarr royal politics — most committed to traditional slaver economics, most resistant to the kind of incremental reform Tash-Murkon and Kor-Azor periodically push. When the Empire needs an institutional voice for 'the old order is non-negotiable', Kador provides it. Strategically aligned with Ardishapur on most religious-political questions; structurally opposed to Tash-Murkon's pragmatic accommodation strategies.

nodes.name: House Ardishapur
nodes.brief: One of the five Amarr royal houses. Slaver-conservative; the house responsible for the Starkmanir genocide via Archon Ardishapur's son Idonis. Damyas III bestowed the Ammatar honorific on the Nefantar.
nodes.master_summary: House Ardishapur carries the historical weight of the Starkmanir genocide — Archon Ardishapur's hardline replacement of the liberal Arzad Hamri provoked the uprising, and Idonis's orbital bombardment annihilated Starkmanir Prime. The Imperian Age reveal that the Ammatar had been hiding Starkmanir survivors throughout was a direct rebuke to Ardishapur's centuries-long claim that the genocide was complete. House Ardishapur's modern political posture is aligned with Kador on conservative slaver economics; its reputational baggage limits its diplomatic reach.

nodes.name: Sansha Kuvakei
nodes.brief: Founder of Sansha's Nation. Megalomaniac who pursued forced cybernetic enslavement under the banner of post-human evolution. Killed in YC36; later resurrected as a True Slave-Master.
nodes.master_summary: Founder of Sansha's Nation. Megalomaniac who pursued forced cybernetic enslavement under the banner of post-human evolution. Killed in YC36, then later resurrected as a True Slave-Master and continues to coordinate Sansha incursions across New Eden. The structural prototype for every later 'transcend humanity through forced merger' antagonist — including the Deathless framing.

nodes.name: Malkalen Disaster
nodes.brief: YC110 Caldari–Federation summit aboard Ishukone Headquarters station at Malkalen. Alexander Noir's Nyx struck the station with safety protocols disabled, killing Otro Gariushi and triggering the State's invasion of Caldari Prime in YC112.
nodes.master_summary: YC110 Caldari–Federation summit aboard Ishukone Headquarters station at Malkalen. Alexander Noir's Nyx struck the station with safety protocols disabled, killing Otro Gariushi and tens of thousands. The 'broker' plot targeted Otro to stop Ishukone's Insorum development. The disaster became Tibus Heth's pretext to militarize the State and triggered the YC112 Caldari invasion of Caldari Prime — the largest interstate war of the modern era.

nodes.name: Triglavian Collective
nodes.brief: Abyssal humanoid civilization that emerged from Abyssal Deadspace. Conquered 27 systems via the Invasion arc to form Pochven. Tied to abyssal-filament tech and the Drifter conflict.
nodes.master_summary: Abyssal humanoid civilization that emerged from Abyssal Deadspace via the Drifter Crisis upheavals. Conquered 27 systems through the Invasion arc to form Pochven. Tied to abyssal-filament technology and a multi-front conflict with the Drifters. Their proxis-class doctrine sits adjacent to Construct-derived tech without using it the same way — making them a third pole next to Drifter and capsuleer civilizations.

nodes.name: Pochven
nodes.brief: Triglavian-controlled region formed from the 27 Invasion-arc conquered systems. Bordered region between Triglavian space and standard New Eden. Site of recent Deathless / Archbine activity.
nodes.master_summary: Triglavian-controlled region formed from the 27 Invasion-arc conquered systems. Bordered space between Triglavian abyssal territory and standard New Eden. Recent Deathless / Archbine activity has used Pochven as a staging zone, and the Zarzakh-to-Pochven corridor is one of the few non-empire transit routes the Deathless Network exploits.

nodes.name: Zarzakh
nodes.brief: The Deathless's home base. Hidden system with EVE-gate-adjacent properties. Coordination center for the Deathless Network.
nodes.master_summary: The Deathless's home base. A hidden system with EVE-gate-adjacent properties revealed during the Equinox-era Deathless arc. The Deathless Network's coordination hub — pirate factions report to Zarzakh, Archbine defectors transit through it, and the Shikari operations are run from it. Sits structurally where Pochven meets the Eve Gate periphery.

nodes.name: Provists (Caldari Provist regime)
nodes.brief: Tibus Heth's authoritarian faction that seized control of the Caldari State after the Malkalen Disaster. Brutal labor / militaristic ideology; rebranded the State along Mountain-faction lines.
nodes.master_summary: Tibus Heth's authoritarian faction that seized de-facto control of the Caldari State after the Malkalen Disaster (YC110). Built around Kaalakiota labor militancy and Mountain-faction ideology, the Provists ran the State through the YC112 invasion of Caldari Prime, the YC115 Caldari–Federation conflict resolution, and into the Empyrean Age. Their downfall — the rise of Akimaka Saraki and the post-Heth consolidation — is the proximate cause of the current Lai-Dai-vs-Ishukone axis.

nodes.name: Caldari Invasion of Caldari Prime
nodes.brief: Massive Caldari State military operation that retook Caldari Prime from Federation control. Heth's signature war, justified as restitution for Malkalen.
nodes.master_summary: The YC112 Caldari State invasion of Caldari Prime — the retaking of the homeworld lost during the Caldari Rebellion. Tibus Heth justified the operation as restitution for the Malkalen Disaster (YC110). Mordu's Legion participated under contract, then later announced departure. The invasion locked in Heth's Provist regime and established the modern Caldari claim on Caldari Prime that the YC128 conflict is once again contesting.

nodes.name: Alexander Noir
nodes.brief: Capsuleer pilot of the Nyx mothership that struck Ishukone Headquarters at Malkalen. Officially named the perpetrator; broker plot evidence suggests he was framed and the safety protocols were disabled by other parties.
nodes.master_summary: Capsuleer pilot of the Nyx mothership that struck Ishukone Headquarters at Malkalen, killing Otro Gariushi and tens of thousands. Officially named the perpetrator of the Malkalen Disaster, but the broker plot evidence — station and ship safety protocols disabled at the same time — suggests he was set up to take the blame for the Insorum-targeted assassination. The structural ambiguity around Noir is what makes Malkalen 'Disaster' canonically rather than 'Attack.'

nodes.name: Aldrith Shutaq
nodes.brief: Khanid noble and Provost-Marshal-era figure mentioned in the Heth coverage. Royal Khanid Navy lineage.
nodes.master_summary: Khanid noble in the Provost-Marshal-era news cycle. Royal Khanid Navy lineage. Frequently surfaces as a Khanid voice in the Pure Faith Harvest / Lost Adama era and the post-Allar-Chakaide Khanid succession.

nodes.name: Caldari Navy
nodes.brief: Caldari State military fleet. Backbone of the YC112 Caldari Prime invasion and the current Caldari conquest of Syndicate.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari State unified military fleet. Backbone of every State military operation since the Empyrean Age — the YC112 Caldari Prime invasion, the YC115 capitulation theater, the Triglavian Invasion response, and the current YC128 Syndicate conquest. Operationally controlled by the Caldari Executive Panel through the Provost / now Saraki authority chain.

nodes.name: Federation Navy
nodes.brief: Gallente Federation military fleet. Conducted the YC124 invasion of Intaki + adjacent constellations that triggered the Uprising arc.
nodes.master_summary: Gallente Federation military fleet. Conducted the YC124 invasion of Intaki + adjacent constellations that triggered the Uprising arc and the constellation deal with the Intaki Syndicate that the YC128 conquest now invalidates. Currently under President Moreau's authority — and the hawkish Senate factions worry her Autonomist-Party origins make her unable to direct it firmly.

nodes.name: Amarr Imperial Navy
nodes.brief: Amarr Empire military fleet. Operates across all five Royal Houses' territories under the Empress's direct authority.
nodes.master_summary: Amarr Empire military fleet. Operates across all five Royal Houses' territories under the Empress's direct authority. Currently engaged in operations against Drifters in Khanid space and against capsuleer disruption of slave-shipment lanes — and Catiz I's territorial reorganization restructured their command chain.

nodes.name: Royal Khanid Navy
nodes.brief: Khanid Kingdom's autonomous military fleet, separate from the Imperial Navy. Repulsed the Pure Faith Harvest raid YC120.
nodes.master_summary: Khanid Kingdom's autonomous military fleet, separate from the Imperial Navy. Carries the Empire's military burden in the southern frontier — repulsed the Pure Faith Harvest raid YC120, conducted the brutal Kahah pacification under Allar Chakaide, and engages Blood Raider incursions on a sustained basis. The 19th Royal Uolans is one of its elite units.

nodes.name: Capitulation at Caldari Prime
nodes.brief: Theater that ended the Caldari–Federation conflict at Caldari Prime. Concluded the Heth-era invasion cycle.
nodes.master_summary: The YC115 capitulation theater that ended the Caldari–Federation conflict at Caldari Prime, concluding the Heth-era invasion cycle that began with the YC112 retake. The terms preserved Caldari sovereignty over Caldari Prime — the settlement the YC128 Syndicate conquest is structurally challenging.

nodes.name: Minmatar Elder War
nodes.brief: YC110 reappearance of the Minmatar Elder Fleet from Anoikis. Triggered Republic invasions of the Ammatar Mandate and Amarr Empire.
nodes.master_summary: The YC110 reappearance of the Minmatar Elder Fleet from Anoikis after centuries of exile. Triggered Republic invasions of the Ammatar Mandate and Amarr Empire, the Empress Jamyl I crisis, and the Sansha capital fleet incident in Jove space. The Elder War is the structural mirror of Heth's YC112 Caldari Prime invasion — both are 'reclamation' wars that reset the four-empire balance.

nodes.name: Seyllin Disaster
nodes.brief: YC111 catastrophic plasma-fire event at Seyllin I that killed everyone on the planet. Triggered the Apocrypha-era wormhole opening and the discovery of Anoikis.
nodes.master_summary: YC111 catastrophic plasma-fire event at Seyllin I that killed everyone on the planet. The Sisters of EVE 'Wormhole Colony Lost' announcement followed. The Seyllin event triggered the Apocrypha-era wormhole opening, the discovery of Anoikis, the Sleeper civilization remnants, and ultimately the Drifter emergence cycle. Every wormhole-tech thread in the warpath board traces back to Seyllin.

nodes.name: Caroline's Star
nodes.brief: YC117 cosmic anomaly — visible new bright object that appeared overnight. Tied to the Drifter emergence and Eve Gate stress events.
nodes.master_summary: YC117 cosmic anomaly — a bright new object that appeared overnight, visible across all four empires simultaneously. The Sisters of EVE issued a statement tying it to the Drifter emergence and Eve Gate stress events. Caroline's Star is structurally the visible signature of the Drifter Hyperspace Network reaching New Eden's normal space — the moment the Drifter conflict moved from Anoikis backstory to current threat.

nodes.name: True Slaves
nodes.brief: Sansha cybernetic forced-merger drones produced from captured slaves. Sansha Kuvakei's signature technology and the True Power Project's product.
nodes.master_summary: Sansha cybernetic forced-merger drones produced from captured slaves via the True Power Project. The pharmacological-and-implant complement to Vitoxin — Sansha's tech weaponizes merger; Amarr's tech weaponizes dependence; Mordu/Ishukone's clone-soldier tech sits structurally between them. Sansha Kuvakei's signature technology and the canonical prototype for every later 'forced post-human' framing including the Deathless arc.

nodes.name: Sleeper Civilization
nodes.brief: Lost Jovian-descended civilization in Anoikis wormhole space. Awakened during the Apocrypha-era wormhole opening; structurally tied to the Construct and the Drifter emergence.
nodes.master_summary: Lost Jovian-descended civilization in Anoikis wormhole space — Jove consciousnesses that committed fully to the Construct and never came out. Awakened during the Apocrypha-era wormhole opening (post-Seyllin). The Sleeper-to-Drifter transition is one of the Drifter Crisis's central canonical mysteries; AIR Labs and SoE both research the boundary.
